speaker: DAUPHIN
text: The sun of heaven, methought, was loath to set,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: But stayed and made the western welkin blush,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: When English measured backward their own ground

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: In faint retire. O, bravely came we off,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: When with a volley of our needless shot,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: After such bloody toil, we bid good night

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And wound our tott’ring colors clearly up,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Last in the field and almost lords of it.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Where is my prince, the Dauphin?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Here. What news?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Count Melun is slain. The English lords,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: By his persuasion, are again fall’n off,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And your supply, which you have wished so long,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Are cast away and sunk on Goodwin Sands.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Ah, foul, shrewd news. Beshrew thy very heart!

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: I did not think to be so sad tonight

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: As this hath made me. Who was he that said

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: King John did fly an hour or two before

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: The stumbling night did part our weary powers?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Whoever spoke it, it is true, my lord.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Well, keep good quarter and good care tonight.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: The day shall not be up so soon as I

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: To try the fair adventure of tomorrow.
